workflow.name: Detect WooCommerce order delays with Gmail and Slack alerts in real time
workflow.description:
# Real-Time WooCommerce Order Delay Detection with Email & Slack Alerts

This n8n workflow listens to real-time WooCommerce order events and automatically detects delivery delays based on the estimated delivery date. When a delay is identified, it proactively notifies the customer via email and alerts internal teams through Slack, ensuring transparency and faster response to fulfillment issues.

### Quick Start – Get This Running Fast

1. Import the workflow JSON into n8n.
2. Connect WooCommerce credentials.
3. Configure Gmail and Slack credentials.
4. Activate the workflow.
5. Start receiving automatic delay alerts in real time.


## What It Does

This workflow proactively monitors WooCommerce orders for delivery delays. It is triggered whenever a new order is created or updated.

The workflow validates order status, checks the estimated delivery date, calculates delay duration and sends notifications if a delay is detected.


## Who’s It For

- WooCommerce Store Owners  
- E-commerce Operations Teams  
- Customer Support Teams  
- Fulfillment & Logistics Teams  


## Requirements

- WooCommerce store with REST API access  
- Gmail account (OAuth enabled)  
- Slack workspace  
- n8n instance (Cloud or Self-hosted)  


## Workflow Steps

1. WooCommerce Trigger  
2. Normalize Order Data  
3. Validate Order Status  
4. Fetch Estimated Delivery Date  
5. Calculate Delay  
6. Send Email Notification  
7. Send Slack Alert  


## Customization

- Modify delay threshold  
- Customize email and Slack message formats  
- Add escalation logic  


## Optional Enhancements

- SMS / WhatsApp alerts  
- Support ticket creation  
- Analytics dashboards  


## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|------|----------|
| No alerts | Verify credentials |
| Wrong delay | Check date format |
| Workflow stops | Ensure ETA exists |


## Support

For advanced automation or customization support, contact **WeblineIndia**.

parameters.content:
## Trigger & Order Intake
### This workflow starts with a WooCommerce Order Update Webhook, which listens to real-time order status changes.

### The incoming payload is normalized to extract only required fields such as order status, customer details, order number and estimated delivery date.
### This ensures the workflow processes clean and consistent data.


### This replaces manual input and enables real-time automation.

## Order Eligibility & Validation
### The workflow validates whether the order is eligible for delay tracking by checking its status.
### Only orders with the following statuses are processed:
### • processing
### • on-hold
### • shipped
### Multiple IF nodes are chained to simulate OR logic.
### The workflow also verifies that an estimated delivery date exists.



### If the order fails any validation, the workflow stops immediately.

### This prevents false alerts and unnecessary processing.

## Delay Calculation & Decision

### - Once the order is validated, a JavaScript logic node calculates the delay by  comparing the current date with the estimated delivery date.
### - The delay is converted into number of days (delayDays).
### - Only orders delayed by one or more days continue further.

### This ensures notifications are sent only for genuine delays.

## Notifications & Visibility
### Customer Notification & Internal Visibility

### For delayed orders, the workflow automatically:
### • Sends an email notification to the customer
### • Sends an internal alert to Slack with order details

### This keeps customers informed and provides internal visibility to support and fulfillment teams.

### The workflow reduces manual follow-ups and improves customer experience.

## How it Works:

### This workflow automatically listens for WooCommerce order updates using a webhook trigger.
Whenever an order status changes, the workflow receives the updated order payload in real time.

### On each execution, the workflow normalizes the incoming order data and validates whether the order is in a state where delays are possible. It then checks for the presence of an estimated delivery date and calculates whether the order is running behind schedule.

### If a delay is detected, the workflow prepares a customer-friendly notification and sends an alert to the internal team via Slack. Orders that are not delayed or do not meet validation criteria are safely ignored.

### Overall, this workflow enables proactive customer communication, reduces support inquiries related to delivery delays and improves operational transparency without manual intervention.

## Setup Steps:

1. Create a WooCommerce Order Update Webhook in n8n to trigger the workflow in real time.

2. Configure the Webhook node to receive POST requests from WooCommerce order updates.

3. Normalize the incoming order payload using a Set / Edit Fields node to extract required fields such as order status, customer email, order number and estimated delivery date.

4. Add chained IF conditions to validate whether the order status is eligible for delay tracking (processing, on-hold or shipped).

5. Add an IF condition to verify that an estimated delivery date exists before performing delay calculations.

6. Use a JavaScript Code node to calculate delay duration by comparing the current date with the estimated delivery date.

7. Add an IF condition to determine whether the order is delayed based on the calculated delay value.

8. Send an automated email notification to the customer when a delay is detected.

9. Send an internal alert to Slack containing order details and delay information for team visibility.

parameters.message: =Hi {{ $('Normalize Order').item.json.customerName }},  Your order is delayed by {{$json.delayDays}} day(s). We’re actively working on it and will update you soon.  Thank you for your patience.
parameters.subject: Update on your order

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
